chapter.label: Tale 111
chapter.title: The Deed Of A Bald Man Dressed In Sack-cloth

paragraphs.text: When I and my companion were in Alexandria, one day we went to the Church of Saint Theodosios. A bald man accosted us who was wearing sack-cloth down to his knees. He scemed to be insane.

Abba Sophronios said 10 me: ‘Give me a coin and you shall see the virtue of this man who is approaching us’. T gave him five coppers photeis which he took and gave to the one who seemed to be insane, who received them without a word. Keeping ourselves out of sight, we followed him. When he liad turned the street-corner, he stretched out his right hand (in which he held the coins) towards heaven, held it up high, and then prostrated himself before God: and went his way, leaving the coins on the ground.
